architecture.summary: A modified Llama model integrated with a program-guided learning framework where predefined logical programs guide the model in applying formal logic operations (conjunction, disjunction, quantification). Logical programs serve as structured constraints during training, channeling the model's outputs through symbolic reasoning pathways to enforce logical consistency across multiple reasoning steps. The framework combines the base language model's generative capabilities with external logical program specifications that structure the reasoning process.

biblio.title: Efficient Logical Reasoning in Large Language Models through Program-Guided Learning

claims.dataset: symbolic logic, mathematical reasoning, and program synthesis benchmarks
claims.statement: The modified Llama model integrated with a program-guided learning framework demonstrates significant improvements in task accuracy, consistency, and error reduction across various logical reasoning benchmarks
The enhanced model outperforms the baseline in both accuracy and efficiency
The approach shows greater resilience in handling ambiguous tasks through probabilistic logic mechanisms
Predefined logical programs guide the model in applying formal logic operations such as conjunction, disjunction, and quantification

Introducing structured guidance into the training process allows models to transcend the limitations of purely statistical approaches, providing a systematic means of enforcing logical consistency across multiple reasoning steps
